summary: Conjecture 4.11 of arXiv:2310.04265 asserts that for any ordered graph $(M,\prec)$ with maximum degree $1$, the class of $(M,\prec)$-free ordered graphs is $\chi$-bounded; this would imply $\overrightarrow{\chi}$-boundedness results for certain tournament classes via the paper's backedge-graph correspondence. No paper resolving this conjecture was found. Two papers citing the source were identified via Semantic Scholar — arXiv:2602.09863 (Crew et al., 2026) on characterising large clique number in tournaments, and arXiv:2407.19270 (Aboulker et al., 2024) on degreewidth of digraphs — but neither could be verified as addressing the ordered-graph chi-boundedness conjecture within the web-call budget.
search_queries: Aboulker Aubian Charbit Lopes ordered graphs chi-bounded maximum degree 2310.04265
ordered graphs chi-bounded M-free matching ordered graph conjecture 2024 2025
notes: Two papers citing arXiv:2310.04265 were located via the Semantic Scholar API: arXiv:2602.09863 (Crew, Fan, Koerts, Moore, Spirkl 2026, 'Characterizing Large Clique Number in Tournaments') and arXiv:2407.19270 (Aboulker, Oijid, Petit, Rocton, Simon 2024, 'Computing the degreewidth of a digraph is hard'). Their full texts were not fetched (5-call cap reached), so it is unknown whether either addresses Conjecture 4.11. The conjecture is a supporting technical statement linking ordered-graph chi-boundedness to tournament chi-bar-boundedness; no direct resolution was found in the indexed literature.
